name: Feature Engineering Pipeline
description: Feature engineering turns raw data into model-ready inputs: records are cleaned and missing values handled, categorical variables encoded, numeric features scaled, and derived features created, then features are selected by importance; transformations are fit only on training data to avoid leakage before producing the feature matrix.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Raw dataset"]
    N2["Clean + handle missing"]
    N3["Encode categoricals"]
    N4["Scale / normalize"]
    N5["Create derived features"]
    N6["Select / reduce features"]
    N7{"Feature importance ok?"}
    N8["Drop or transform"]
    N9["Prevent leakage (train fit)"]
    N10["Feature matrix ready"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 -->|no| N8
    N8 -->|iterate| N5
    N7 -->|yes| N9
    N9 --> N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Feature Engineering and Selection: A Practical Approach for Predictive Models
Hands-On Machine Learning with Scikit-Learn, Keras, and TensorFlow (2nd ed.)

notes: Source-grounded extraction (v2): node content derived from cited protocol literature with per-node provenance; topology reflects the actual procedure.
